methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Photoroom, an AI & Software company founded by Matthieu Rouif and Eliot Andres, is in Series B having raised $43M to date against a current post-money valuation of $500M. The peer universe consists of 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, and Nominal) alongside 1 public reference point (Palantir), providing a mixed but sector-aligned comp set.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x applied to the $500M last post-money, implies a forward valuation of $950M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-stage AI & Software private companies yields a median valuation of $5.3B, though the range spans $1.0B to $32.0B, reflecting significant dispersion driven by Safe Superintelligence's $32.0B outlier position.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $808M (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $3.1B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively near current valuation, while peer-median reflects the elevated multiples present in contemporaneous Series B AI fundraising. Key uncertainties include Photoroom's positioning relative to the outlier-heavy peer set and whether its product-market trajectory supports the upper range. A central estimate near $3.1B aligns with mid-tier peers but requires validation against unit economics and growth metrics not present in available data.
